input.background: We calculate the $95 \%$ confidence interval for $\mu_1-\mu_2$ for the cholesterol illustrative data (see Table 12.5).
input.question: Calculate the standard error of the difference between the sample means: $S E_{\bar{x}_1-\bar{x}_2}=\sqrt{\frac{s_1^2}{n_1}+\frac{s_2^2}{n_2}}$.

s1_squared.description: The variance of the first sample, used in the calculation of the standard error.

n1.description: The number of observations in the first sample, used in the calculation of the standard error.

s2_squared.description: The variance of the second sample, used in the calculation of the standard error.

n2.description: The number of observations in the second sample, used in the calculation of the standard error.

SE_difference.value: Standard error of the difference between sample means
SE_difference.description: The standard error calculated from the variances and sample sizes of two samples, representing the uncertainty of the difference between sample means.
